post.description:
Launch a new Workflow synchronously. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createWorkflows`

get.description:
List all workflows in your Ironclad account. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readWorkflows`

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter the workflows that are listed based on their status. If this parameter is omitted, `active` workflows will be returned. Active workflows include workflows in the Create, Review, Sign, and Archive stages. Also accepts multiple comma-separated statuses to get workflows in more than one status.

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: Filter workflows to a specific Template ID.

parameters.name: lastUpdated
parameters.description: Retrieve workflows that have been updated since a UTC date.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Filter workflows using a formula. The workflow attribute ID should be enclosed in brackets `[ ]` and the value should be enclosed in single quotes `' '`. Workflow attributes for a specific workflow design can be identified using the [List All Workflow Schemas](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-workflow-schemas) endpoint. Supported formula operations include:
  • Equals
  • Contains
  • And
  • Or

post.description:
Launch a new Workflow asynchronously for non-blocking performance, which is helpful when/if you provide files to the Workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createWorkflows`

get.summary: Retrieve the Status of an Async Workflow Create Job
get.description:
Check the status of a Workflow you created while using the [Create a Workflow Async](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/create-a-workflow-sync-vs-async#create-a-new-workflow-async) route. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createWorkflows`

parameters.name: asyncJobId
parameters.description: The identifier provided in the response of creating a Workflow asynchronously.

get.description:
View the data associated with a specific workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readWorkflows`

Returns a list of approvals for the workflow. The `approvalGroups` property will display only triggered approvals (i.e. conditional approvals that have not been triggered will not appear). 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readApprovals`

title.example: Consulting Agreement with Michael Scott Paper Company

status.description: The status of the reviewer. If the status is `approved`, the reviewer has approved. If the status is `pending`, the reviewer has not yet approved.

The status of the approval group. If the status is `active`, the approval group is currently reviewing and its reviewers may approve. If the status is `approved`, all reviewers in the approval group have approved. If the status is `pending`, the approval group is waiting for prior groups to approve and its reviewers cannot approve.

order.description: The order in which the approval group is executed. Only relevant if there is more than one approval group and sequential approvals are enabled.

get.summary: Retrieve the Approval Requests on a Workflow
get.description:
Returns a list of approval requests that have taken place on the workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readApprovals`

parameters.name: actorDetails
parameters.description: An optional boolean parameter that adds additional information about the actor to each item in the response. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: requestType
parameters.description: An optional string parameter that specifies the type of approval requests and returns approval requests by `role` or `user`.

startTime.description: Timestamp when the approval request became available for review

endTime.description: Timestamp when the approval request was approved or interrupted by workflow actions

status.description: Current state of an approval request that indicates whether the request is approved, in progress, or affected by workflow actions.

actorId.description: User ID to whom the approval request is assigned to

role.description: Role ID of the role assigned to the user for the approval request

roleName.description: Display name of the role assigned to the user for the approval request

duration.description: Duration of the completed approval request in milliseconds

aggregateDuration.description: Total duration of the completed approval request, obtained by adding the duration of the approved request with the durations of preceding requests (if any) where the approver was able to review but interrupted by a workflow pause.

patch.description:
Updates an approval to the specified status. Approvals can only be updated during the Review step and when a given approval group is active (when it is that group's turn to approve). 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.updateApprovals`

parameters.name: roleId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the approver role whose status should be changed. This identifier can be retrieved using the `GET /workflows/{id}/approvals` endpoint.

schema.description: Note that `user` is required when approving with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when approving via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

user.description: The Ironclad user approving the workflow. The user must be currently assigned as the approver.

type.description: The mechanism of identifying the Ironclad user's account.

enum: This role does not exist in this workflow
This approval either was not requested or this user was not assigned to this approval
message.example: This role does not exist in this workflow

enum: This approval cannot be approved out of order
message.example: This approval cannot be approved out of order

get.summary: Retrieve the Turn History on a Workflow
get.description:
An array of objects for each turn on a workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readTurnHistory`

Returns information about a workflow in the sign step. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readSignStatus` 

**NOTE:** The workflow must be in the sign step to access this route.

message.example: This workflow is not at the Sign step, and is currently at the Review step

post.description:
Send a signature request.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.sendSignatureRequests`

 **Note**: You can only send a packet out for signature if all signers have an associated signature or initials tag (tag requirements are dependent on the signature provider).

Cancel a signature request that was out for signature.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.cancelSignatureRequests`

reason.description: The reason for canceling the signature request.

patch.description:
Reassign a signer or edit an external signer's name or email, as specified by the `signerRoleName` parameter. The original signer will receive an email that the packet has been reassigned if it was sent out for signature.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.updateSigners`

**NOTE**: Editing a signer will not cancel a signature request unless your configured signature provider is Adobe Sign. Also, you cannot update a signer that has already signed via the API for any signature provider.

parameters.name: signerRoleName
parameters.description: The role name of the signer to be updated. Note: the role name should be URL encoded in the URL.

delete.description:
Deletes a signer from a signature packet, as specified by the `signerRoleName` parameter.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.deleteSigners`

**NOTE**: Removing a signer will cancel the current packet that is out collecting signatures.

parameters.name: signerRoleName
parameters.description: The role name of the signer to be removed. Note: the role name should be URL encoded in the URL.

post.description:
Send a reminder email to a signer that a signature is needed.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.remindSigners`

**NOTE**: You can only send a reminder after the signature packet has been sent out for signature. Also, if using Adobe Sign as your signature provider, reminding a signer reminds all unsigned signers.

parameters.name: signerRoleName
parameters.description: The role name of the signer to be reminded. Note: the role name should be URL encoded in the URL.

message.example: You cannot remind the signer at this time (you can only send one reminder per hour). Please try again later.

get.description:
Returns a list of workflow signers and the status of their signature. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readSignatures`

message.example: This workflow is not at the Sign step, and is currently at the Review step

post.summary: Create a Recipient URL for a Signer
post.description:
Create a recipient URL for a signer or viewer to access the signature request.

This functionality is exclusive to Ironclad Signature. See [this help center article](https://support.ironcladapp.com/hc/en-us/articles/12276957992983-Manage-Signatures-During-the-Sign-Step#copysignerlink) for more information.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createSignatureRecipientUrls`

type.description: The type of recipient.

roleName.description: The role name of the signer. A full list of role names can be located using the [List All Workflow Signers](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-workflow-signers) endpoint.

type.description: The type of recipient.

email.description: The email address of the CC recipient.

url.description: The URL for the recipient to access the signature request.

message.example: invalid recipient type; *, signer, viewer are acceptable recipient types
user is not authorized to create signature request URL for signature request, please try again later
user cannot create signature request URL for recipient at this time

post.summary: Create an Embeddable Recipient URL for a Signer
post.description:
Create an embeddable recipient URL for a signer to access the signature request.

This functionality is exclusive to Ironclad Signature. See [this help center article](https://support.ironcladapp.com/hc/en-us/articles/12276957992983-Manage-Signatures-During-the-Sign-Step#copysignerlink) for more information.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createEmbeddableSignerUrls`

type.description: The type of recipient.

roleName.description: The role name of the signer. A full list of role names can be located using the [List All Workflow Signers](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-workflow-signers) endpoint.

frameAncestor.description: The hostname of the parent frame that the recipient URL can be embedded in. Can optionally include a URL scheme and/or port number. Wildcards (i.e. '*') are not allowed.

allowMultipleUsages.description: Whether or not the embeddable recipient URL can be used multiple times before it expires. Defaults to false if not set.

url.description: The embeddable URL for the recipient to access the signature request. The URL can only be embedded in a parent matching the host specified in frameAncestor.

message.example: invalid recipient type; signer is the only acceptable recipient type
user is not authorized to create signature request URL for signature request, please try again later
user cannot create signature request URL for recipient at this time

post.summary: Create a Signed Document on a Workflow
post.description:
Upload a fully or partially signed document to a specified workflow that is in sign step. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.uploadSignedDocuments`

attachment.description: Must be a binary. Base64 is not accepted at this time.

metadata.description: A JSON string that contains all the metadata for the Document.

signerRoleNames.description: If the document does not contain all the required signatures, use this field to specify which signers have already signed on the document. If it's left blank then it's assumed to be a fully signed document.

items.description: The role name of the signers. A full list of role names can be located using the [List All Workflow Signers](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-workflow-signers) endpoint.

message.example: Detected virus in one or more of the files received.
The signer field contains invalid signers, please check.

get.description:
Returns a list of workflow participants. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readParticipants`

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The Ironclad user's email address.

patch.summary: Revert a Workflow to the Review Step
patch.description:
Reverts a workflow to the Review step. Only workflows sourced from Workflow Designer and in the Sign step can be reverted. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.revertToReview`

schema.description: Note that `user` is required when reverting with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when reverting via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

user.description: The Ironclad user reverting the workflow to the Review step. The user must have access to the workflow.

type.description: The mechanism of identifying the Ironclad user's account.

enum: workflow was not built in workflow designer and cannot be reverted to review via the API
workflow cannot be reverted to review as it is not in the sign stage
message.example: workflow cannot be reverted to review as it is not in the sign stage
user is not a participant on the workflow

post.description:
Cancel a workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.cancel`

Pause an active workflow.

message.example: Only active workflows in the review step can be paused.

post.description:
Resume a paused Workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.pauseAndResume`

message.example: Only paused workflows in the review step can be resumed.

post.summary: Create a Comment on a Workflow
post.description: Creates a comment in the specified workflow's activity feed.

schema.description: Note that `creator` is required when commenting with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when commenting via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

repliedToActivityFeedMessageId.description: Use to specify the comment to reply to

post.summary: Create a Comment on a Workflow
post.description:
Creates a comment in the specified workflow's activity feed. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createComments`

schema.description: Note that `user` is required when commenting with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when commenting via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

comment.description: The comment to be added to the workflow activity feed. To @-mention a user, use the format `<@john.doe@example.com>`.

repliedToActivityFeedMessageId.description: Use to specify a comment to reply to. Message ID can be found via the [List All Comments on a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-comments-in-a-workflow) endpoint.

get.summary: List all Comments on a Workflow
get.description:
Return a list of comments on a workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readComments`

get.summary: List a Comment from a Specified Workflow
get.description:
Return a single comment for a specified workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readComments`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a workflow.

parameters.name: commentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a comment.

patch.description:
The workflow must be in the Review step in order for its data to be updated. Use the `remove` action to clear field values and the `set` action to add or modify values. Form validation is enforced; required fields cannot be removed and any required fields triggered by conditions must be populated.

**Note:** This endpoint does not support updating document/file type attributes. To manage documents, use the document-specific endpoints.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.updateWorkflows`

action.description: The type of action you'd like to take on an existing attribute (read-only fields cannot be updated). Use the remove action to clear values. If using the remove action, the attribute cannot be required by the workflow.

path.description: The workflow attribute id that you'd like to make a change to. Workflow attribute IDs and values can be retrieved using the [Retrieve a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/retrieve-a-workflow) endpoint.

value.description: The value to change the attribute to. Only required when the `action` property is `set` and not `remove`. The value's format must conform to the field type (e.g., `"someString"` for strings, `true` or `false` for booleans, etc.

comment.description: A comment that explains the updates you are making to the workflow.

propertyKey.description: The unique identifier for the property.

readOnly.description: Determines whether the property's value can be updated.

propertyKey.description: The unique identifier for the property.

readOnly.description: Determines whether the property's value can be updated.

propertyKey.description: The unique identifier for the property.

readOnly.description: Determines whether the property's value can be updated.

value.message: No information changed. The updates provided did not introduce any new values
non-existent attribute(s) specified: someRandomAttribute

invalidParamFormValidationFailed.description: Form validation may fail if (1) a required conditional field is triggered but not populated by your request (2) the value does not conform to the expected field type (e.g., sending strings to number fields).

value.message: form validation failed, please check schema
Cannot patch read-only attribute someRandomFunctionAttribute

get.description:
Retrieve all documents from a workflow that are included and excluded from the signature packet. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readDocuments`

signaturePacketDocuments.description: The signature packet documents in the order they are displayed on the packet.

excludedFromSignaturePacketDocuments.description: The documents that are excluded from the signature packet.

patch.summary: Update Signature Packet on a Workflow
patch.description:
Update a workflow's signature packet by moving documents into it, moving documents out of it, or reordering documents within it. The request body specifies which workflow documents should be in the signature packet and their order. Documents in the request but not currently in the signature packet will be moved into it. Documents currently in the signature packet but not in the request will be moved out of it.

**Note:** Only editable file types (.doc, .docx, .pdf) can be included in the signature packet.

**Restrictions:**
- Workflow must be at Review stage 
- All document keys must be valid editable documents on the workflow 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.updateSignaturePacket`

signaturePacketDocumentKeys.description: Array of document keys representing the desired signature packet order. Only editable file types (.doc, .docx, .pdf) are valid. Cannot be empty if the workflow has a sign step configured.

signaturePacketDocuments.description: The signature packet documents in the updated order.

excludedFromSignaturePacketDocuments.description: The documents that are excluded from the signature packet.

post.summary: Create a Document on a Workflow
post.description:
Create a document in the specified workflow attribute. If a document key to an existing draft document is provided, the new file will be uploaded as a new version of the existing document. For all other non-draft documents and when document key is absent, this operation will only append the new document to the document attribute array. Please note that to upload a signed document, reference the [Create a Signed Document on a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/workflow-upload-signed-document) endpoint.

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.createDocuments`

parameters.name: attributeId
parameters.description: The file attribute (e.g. "draft", "myFileField") - "document" type attributes can be found in the schema portion of the [Retrieve a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/retrieve-a-workflow) response.

attachment.description: Must be a binary. Base64 is not accepted at this time.

metadata.description: A JSON string that contains all the metadata for the Document.

key.description: The unique identifier for the document. This key can be located using the [Retrieve a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/retrieve-a-workflow) endpoint. Should only be provided when creating a new version of an existing draft document. Versioning is not supported for non-draft documents.

get.description:
Download a document associated with a specific workflow via a reference to its document key. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readDocuments`

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the attachment. This key can be located using the [Retrieve a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/retrieve-a-workflow) endpoint. In the response, locate the file attribute (e.g., `draft`) and look at its `download` parameter. The download parameter includes the key at the end of the URL `.../document/{ATTACHMENT_KEY}/download`

get.summary: Retrieve Email Threads from A Workflow
get.description:
List all email threads in the specified workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readEmailCommunications`

get.summary: Retrieve an Email Thread from a Specified Workflow
get.description:
List a single email thread for a specified workflow. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readEmailCommunications`

get.summary: Retrieve an Attachment from an Email Thread
get.description:
Retrieve an attachment from the specified email thread. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readEmailCommunications`

parameters.name: attachmentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the attachment. This key can be located using the [Retrieve email threads from workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/retrieve-emails) endpoint. In the response, locate the file attribute (e.g., `attachments`) and look at its `download` parameter. The download parameter includes the key at the end of the URL `...emails/{emailThreadId}/attachment/{ATTACHMENT_ID}`

get.description:
Returns a list of workflow schemas. Each schema specifies the fields used in the workflow's launch form. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.workflows.readSchemas`

parameters.name: form
parameters.description: The launch form is the only form supported at this time.

get.description:
Returns the fields used in the workflow's launch form.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of a schema (see explanation of [Template ID](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/getting-started-api)). A list of identifiers can be retrieved using the `GET /workflow-schemas` endpoint. Only published workflows will have an identifier.
parameters.name: id

get.description:
View all records in the company, with filtering available via query parameters.

parameters.name: sortField
parameters.description: The field to sort Records. Only one field is supported in the sort operation.

parameters.name: sortDirection

post.description:
Create a contract record by specifying its intended metadata properties. See [more documentation](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/docs/create-a-record-1) on creating a record. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.records.createRecords`

type.description: The type of record to be created. You can view available record types by retrieving records metadata.

properties.description: Object containing key/value pairs of metadata attached to the record. The key you specify must have a value with a supported type as outlined in the Supported Property Types section.

contractValue.description: The monetary value of the contract.

children.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the records to be set as child records of the current record.

parameters.name: importId

oneOf.description: If `importId` was not provided, the response will be a single object.

oneOf.title: Array of Prediction Statuses

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or Ironclad ID of the Record.

type.description: The type of record to be created. You can view available record types by retrieving records metadata.

properties.description: Object containing key/value pairs of metadata attached to the record. The key you specify must have a value with a supported type as outlined in the Supported Property Types section.

children.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the records to be set as child records of the current record.

Delete an existing record.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or Ironclad ID of the Record.

patch.description:
Update specific fields on a record. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.records.updateRecords`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or Ironclad ID of the Record.

type.description: The type of Record.

addProperties.description: A key:value map of properties to add. The two below are examples.

removeProperties.description: List of record property ids to be removed (e.g., ['counterpartyAddress', 'agreementExpirationDate'])

addLinks.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the records to link. For an explanation of Record IDs or Ironclad IDs and how to find them, see [Getting Started](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/getting-started-api).

removeLinks.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the record links to remove. For an explanation of Record IDs or Ironclad IDs and how to find them, see [Getting Started](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/getting-started-api).

addChildren.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the records to be set as child records of the current record.

removeParent.description: Boolean flag to indicate if the parent id should be removed for the current record.

removeChildren.description: List of objects containing the Record IDs or Ironclad IDs of the child records to be removed from the current record.

removePropertiesToAmend.description: List of property IDs to stop deriving from this amendment record to the parent record. Removes existing derived property relationships, allowing the parent record to revert to its non-amended value for those fields.

message.example: reason why something has gone wrong

get.description:
View the schema associated with contract records, including available record types and metadata properties. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.records.readSchemas`

post.summary: Create an Attachment on a Record
post.description:
Create an attachment on a specified record at the specified attachment key. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.records.createAttachments`

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID or Ironclad ID of the Record. For an explanation of Record IDs or Ironclad IDs and how to find them, see [Getting Started](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/getting-started-api).

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: The attachment key at which to store the attachment. This can be any arbitrary string.

post.description:
Creates a webhook for the specified events. Send separate requests if you need to create webhooks for multiple target URLs. Each target URL may only have one active registration. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.webhooks.createWebhooks`

events.description: The event type(s) to trigger the webhook. Note: you can use `*` to receive events for all events, although we don't recommend this path, especially for environments with higher volume.

targetURL.description: The URL to send the webhook event payload to. It must be an HTTPS url.

status.description: The status of the webhook. If the status is `disabled`, the webhook will not be triggered.

get.description:
View all webhooks associated with a specific company with filtering available via query parameters. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.webhooks.readWebhooks`

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.webhooks.readWebhooks`

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.webhooks.updateWebhooks`

events.description: The type of events that will trigger a notification to the target URL.

targetURL.description: The URL of where the notification gets sent.

status.description: The status of the webhook. If the status is `disabled`, the webhook will not be triggered.

delete.description:
Delete a specific webhook. 

**OAuth Scope required:** `public.webhooks.deleteWebhooks`

get.description: View the verification key for webhook security implementations

LaunchedWorkflow.title: Launched Workflow
LaunchedWorkflow.description: The response from a successful workflow launch.

id.description: The ID of the launched workflow.

ironcladId.description: The Ironclad ID of the launched workflow.

title.description: The name used for the launched workflow.

step.description: The step the workflow is currently on.

attributes.description: The workflow attributes (also known as properties)

isCancelled.description: Displays if the launched workflow has been cancelled.

isComplete.description: Displays if the launched workflow has been completed.

status.description: The current status of the launched workflow.

created.description: The date (ISO8601 format) the workflow was launched

lastUpdated.description: The date (ISO8601 format) the workflow was last updated

url.description: The url where signature information is located if it exists.

items.description: Ids of records created by the workflow.

isRevertibleToReview.description: Displays if the launched workflow can be reverted back to the review step.

WorkflowLaunchModel.description: Note that `creator` is required when launching with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when launching via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

WorkflowLaunchMultipartModel.description: Note that `creator` is required when launching with a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when launching via an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or 'x-as-user-email` header will be used.

WorkflowResponseModel.description: The response from a successful workflow retrieval.

propertyKey.description: The unique identifier for the property.

readOnly.description: Determines whether the property's value can be updated.

version.description: The version ID of the document's latest version

versionNumber.description: The latest version number of the document

filename.description: The name of the document with the extension

key.description: The unique key associated with the document

download.description: The URL to download the document omitting the base URL

lastModified.description: The timestamp and author of the last modification to the document

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the last modification to the document (ISO 8601 format)

author.description: The author of the last modification to the document

AttributesModel.description: The data that will be used to populate the workflow's fields. Learn more about the various attributes and their associated types by viewing the [Launch a Workflow](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/docs/launch-a-workflow) guide. Your attributes will differ and include more than this simple example.

draft.description: The files for the draft documents of this workflow, please note that we only support `url` for json request and `multipartField` for multipart request

paperSource.description: Indicate the type of paper source used on a contract that supports both templatized and third party paper functionality. This field is required when there exists a question for selecting paper source on the template

oneOf.description: Provide a URL for a file representing the first uploaded version of a counterparty paper. This field is required when the template source is set to third party paper
Provide a file with the same key as in the entry in form-data's attachment, for example, we expect a key called <SampleNDA>

InboundEmailAttachment.title: Inbound Email Attachment
InboundEmailAttachment.description: An attachment on an inbound email

filename.description: The name of the attachment file

download.description: The public API URL to download the attachment (does not include the base URL)

content-type.description: The content type of the attachment

documentKey.description: The document key of the associated document when the attachment has been added to the workflow. Will only be present when the "Add to Workflow" button has been clicked on the activity feed item.

documentVersion.description: The version of the associated document when the attachment has been added to the workflow. Will only be present when the "Add to Workflow" button has been clicked on the activity feed item.

AddUsersToWorkflowModel.description: Must be set to true if mentioning users who are not participants on the workflow.

message.description: The comment text to add, accepts user mentions in the form of <@user_id_or_email>.

CreatorModel.description: The Ironclad user (must be a user in your Ironclad account) to be used for launching the workflow. This is required when using a legacy bearer token but will be ignored when using an OAuth token. If the token was generated with the Authorization Code grant, the associated token user will be used. If the token was generated with the Client Credentials grant, the required `x-as-user-id` or `x-as-user-email` header will be used.
CreatorModel.title: creator

type.description: The type of value used to identify the user.

email.description: The email address of the Ironclad user.

type.description: The type of value used to identify the user.

id.description: The user ID of the Ironclad user.

subject.example: Re: Contract Review Request - New Jedi Interceptors

I'll review the contract with care, ensuring it aligns with the Jedi Order's principles. You'll have my insights soon so we can secure a fleet worthy of peace and balance.

May the Force be with you,
Obi-Wan Kenobi

items.description:
Signer delegates are individuals who are authorized to sign a document on behalf of the original signer. 

 NOTE: Signer delegation is only available with Adobe Sign.

routingOrder.description:
Indicates the order in which a signer signs when signing is sequential. 

 NOTE: If a signer signs with wet signature, their routing order is denoted as -1.

type.description: Indicates an internal Ironclad user.

userId.description: The user ID associated with the signer.

type.description: Indicates an external signer.

signers.description: All signers associated with the workflow.

routing.description: Indicates if routing is sequential (signatures collected in order) or parallel (all signers receive request simultaneously).

type.description: The action type to run on the contract.

standard_terminationNoticePeriod.description: This is the number of days of notice that needs to be given before this agreement can be terminated.

effectiveDate.description: Effective date is when this agreement begins and is considered active. If effective date is not defined then the executed date of the agreement is used to mark the contract as active.

type.description: The action type to run on the contract.

standard_supersedingRecordId.description: The record ID for the contract that has superseded this contract.

type.description: The action type to run on the contract.
The action type to run on the contract.

standard_terminatedDate.description: The date when the contract was terminated.

standard_terminationEffectiveDate.description: The effective date of the contract termination.

standard_terminatingRecordId.description: The record ID for the contract that terminated this contract.

type.description: The action type to run on the contract.
The action type to run on the contract.
The action type to run on the contract.

standard_undoObligationAction.description: The action type that is being undone by an undo action.

standard_renewalType.description:
The renewal type for an agreement will govern what happens on its expiration date if it has one. Accepted values for renewal type:

    	Auto-Renew: These agreements will automatically renew on their expiration date which means they will then have a new expiration date which would be the current date plus the renewal term length.

    	Optional Extension: These agreements have an optional extension which means that the party can choose to renew the agreement by the renewal term length without renegotiating the contract. There may be a notification requirement to exercise this option.

    	Evergreen: These are agreements that don't have an expiration date, which means they are active forever. Also known as perpetual agreements.

    	None: These agreements that don't have a renewal provision and will expire on their expiration date. If an agreement has no renewal provision but also doesn't have an expiration date, it'll be active forever.

    	Other: For renewal provisions that are not covered by these options.

otherRenewalType.description: This property helps downstream reporting for when you have a renewal type not defined by the system.

standard_initialTermLength.description: The initial term is the time from the effective date that the agreement will be effective for. If not defined, then the agreement will be active until the expiration date. If an expiration date is also not defined then the agreement will be active indefinitely, until the status of the agreement is manually changed.

expirationDate.description: Expiration date is when this agreement will end or become inactive. If an expiration date isn't present then the effective date plus the initial term length are used to expire the contract.

standard_renewalsRemaining.description: The number of renewals an auto-renewal or optional extension with limited renewals has left.

standard_renewalOptOutDate.description: An agreement may have an opt-out date that lets you opt-out of the upcoming auto-renewal before it renews.

standard_renewalOptOutPeriod.description: An agreement may have an opt-out period that lets you opt-out of the upcoming auto-renewal before it renews. In the case of an opt period the last day to opt-out is the expiration date minus the opt-out period.

LinkType.description: Identifies the type of relationship this child record has with its parent (e.g. amendment)

RecordParentModel.description: Object containing Record IDs or Ironclad IDs to be set as the parent of the current record.

recordId.description: The ID or Ironclad ID (readable ID) of the record to set as parent

amendmentType.description: How to amend the property value. Currently only 'replace' is supported.

PropertiesToAmend.description: Configuration specifying which properties should be amended from this amendment record to its parent. Keys are property names, values define the amendment behavior. Only valid when parentLinkType is 'amendment'.

metadataProperties.description: Array of available custom metadata properties to include in export. Contact support for assistance on locating your custom metadata properties.

eventCategories.description: Array of categories of audit log events to include in the export.

startDate.description: Starting date (ISO8601 format) of data to be included in the export. Example: `2016-11-17T00:37:22.318Z`

endDate.description: Starting date (ISO8601 format) of data to be included in the export. Example: `2016-11-17T00:37:22.318Z`

exportFormat.description: File format for the export. CSV is the default.

signedUrlEnabled.description: An optional boolean value to enable a signed url for downloading the exported file. If not provided, defaults to false.

exportConfigs.description: Each `exportType` may have additional export configurations specific to this type of export. Use one of the following `exportConfigs` schemas:

ActivityFeedAuthor.title: Activity Feed Author
ActivityFeedAuthor.description: The author of an activity feed item

InternalActivityFeedAuthor.title: Internal Activity Feed Author
InternalActivityFeedAuthor.description: An internal author of an activity feed item

type.description: The type of author

companyName.description: The company name of the author

displayName.description: The display name of the author

userId.description: The user ID of the author

ExternalActivityFeedAuthor.title: External Activity Feed Author
ExternalActivityFeedAuthor.description: An external author of an activity feed item (typically a counterparty)

type.description: The type of author

companyName.description: The company name of the author

displayName.description: The display name of the author

IntegrationActivityFeedAuthor.title: Integration Activity Feed Author
IntegrationActivityFeedAuthor.description: An integration author of an activity feed item

type.description: The type of author

displayName.description: The display name of the integration

SystemActivityFeedAuthor.title: System Activity Feed Author
SystemActivityFeedAuthor.description: A system author of an activity feed item

type.description: The type of author

displayName.description: The display name of the system

ActivityFeedComment.title: Activity Feed Comment
ActivityFeedComment.description: A comment-type item on the workflow activity feed

id.description: Unique ID of the comment-type activity feed item

commentMessage.example: Do or do not. There is no try.

timestamp.description: When the comment was posted (ISO 8601 format)

isExternal.description: Whether the comment was made by an external user

repliedTo.description: The comment that this comment is a reply to

item.description: The ID of the comment that this comment is a reply to

mentionedUserDetails.description: The users mentioned in the comment

id.description: The user ID of the mentioned user

displayName.description: The display name of the mentioned user

email.description: The email of the mentioned user

addedParticipants.description: The users added to the workflow by the comment

items.description: The user display name of the added participant

reactions.description: The emoji reactions to the comment

emojiId.description: The ID of the emoji reaction

reactors.description: The users who reacted with the particular emoji

WebhookEvent.description: Events that can be subscribed to using a webhook.

WebhookModel.description: Webhook object describing the subscribed events and target URL to be called.

events.description: Array of events to which webhook is subscribed to.

targetURL.description: Target URL to which the webhook is called.

companyId.description: Unique ID of the Ironclad company instance to which the webhook belongs to.

status.description: The status of the webhook. If the status is `disabled`, the webhook will not be triggered.

statusLastUpdatedAt.description: The timestamp of the most recent status change in ISO 8601 format.

statusLastUpdatedBy.description: The user or system ID that made the most recent status change.

consecutiveFailureCount.description: The number of consecutive failed deliveries of the webhook. After a certain number of consecutive failures over a certain period of time, the webhook will be disabled. See [more](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/webhook-deactivation).

firstConsecutiveFailure.description: The timestamp of the first consecutive failure of the webhook in ISO 8601 format.

message.example: invalid event type; *, workflow_launched, workflow_updated, workflow_cancelled, workflow_completed, workflow_approval_status_changed, workflow_attribute_updated, workflow_changed_turn, workflow_comment_reaction_added, workflow_comment_reaction_removed, workflow_comment_added, workflow_comment_removed, workflow_comment_updated, workflow_counterparty_invite_sent, workflow_counterparty_invite_revoked, workflow_documents_added, workflow_documents_removed, workflow_documents_updated, workflow_documents_renamed, workflow_document_edited, workflow_paused, workflow_resumed, workflow_signature_packet_sent, workflow_signature_packet_uploaded, or workflow_signature_packet_cancelled, workflow_signer_added, workflow_signer_removed, workflow_signer_reassigned are acceptable event types
webhook already exists for event workflow_completed and target [SOME_EXISTING_TARGET_URL]

browserUrl.description: The URL for accessing the workflow in a browser.

apiUrl.description: The URL for accessing workflow data via the REST API.

WorkflowAsyncLaunchSuccessResponse.description: Response for a successfully asynchronously launched workflow.

error.description: An error object indicating why the workflow failed to launch.

code.description: A status code to identify why the workflow cannot be launched.

message.description: A message indicating why the workflow cannot be launched.

param.description: The required or expected parameter(s) that is missing or invalid and preventing the workflow from being launched.

redirectUrl.description: The URL for accessing the launch form to complete the workflow launch.

WorkflowSchemasResponseRequiredField.description: Indicates whether the field is required. Possible values are 'always', 'never', or 'conditional'. Conditional properties are either gated by a condition on the workflow template, or appear multiple times in the workflow and are only sometimes required.

values.description: An array of choices for dropdowns or multiple-choice questions. Each item represents a valid option. If there are multiple instances of the same property, the options will be combined.

freeTextAllowed.description: Indicates if a free text input is allowed in addition to the dropdown choices.

WorkflowSchemasResponseOptionsField.description: Defines the options for a field, including dropdown choices and whether free text input is allowed.

oneOf.description: A string value from fields such as text inputs, dropdowns, emails, or dates.
An object value from fields such as monetary fields, addresses, or durations.
An array value from fields such as tables.

items.description: An object representing an item in the list. Contains key value pairs of properties from the table

WorkflowSchemasResponseDefaultField.description: Defines the available default value for a field. In the case of multiple instances of the same property, the last evaluated default will be used.

code.description: A unique code to identify why the contract action cannot be run

message.description: A message indicating why the contract action cannot be run

param.description: The required or expected parameter(s) that may be missing in order to run the contract action

code.description: A unique code to identify why the workflow cannot be launched.

message.description: A message indicating why the workflow cannot be launched.

param.description: The required or expected parameter(s) that may be missing in order to launch the workflow.

BadRequestErrorRecordNotFound.description: Bad request error. The specified record does not exist.
BadRequestErrorRecordNotFound.title: Bad Request Error - Record Does not Exist

message.example: couldn't find a record matching the id workflow:6346f76fb8fc01d2b43e7c15a

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentExtension.description: Bad request error. The specified filename does not include a supported extension.
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentExtension.title: Bad Request Error - Record Attachment Extension Not Supported

message.example: filename "NDA - Acme LLC" does not have an accepted extension. Only the following extensions are allowed: .adoc, .asice, .bdoc, .csv, .ddoc, .doc, .docx, .edoc, .eml, .gif, .htm, .jpeg, .jpg, .msg, .pdf, .png, .ppt, .pptx, .rtf, .tif, .tif, .txt, .xls, .xlsx, .xml

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentAlreadyEx.description: Bad request error. The specified attachment key has an existing file.
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentAlreadyEx.title: Bad Request Error - Record Attachment Already Exists.

message.example: record attachment key 'signedCopy' already exists for record

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentUnknownAt.description: Bad request error. Unknown record attachment key.
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentUnknownAt.title: Bad Request Error - Unknown Record Attachment Key

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentUnknownCo.description: Bad request error. Unknown content type.
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentUnknownCo.title: Bad Request Error - Unknown Content Type

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentMissingFo.description: Bad request error. Missing form-data part.
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentMissingFo.title: Bad Request Error - Missing Form-Data Part

BadRequestErrorRecordIDNotFound.description: Record ID Not Found Request Error
BadRequestErrorRecordIDNotFound.title: Bad Request Error - Record ID Not Found

message.example: couldn't find a record matching the id [SOME_NONEXISTENT_RECORD_ID]

BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentIDNotFoun.description: Record Attachment ID Not Found Request Error
BadRequestErrorRecordAttachmentIDNotFoun.title: Bad Request Error - Record Attachment ID Not Found

message.example: couldn't find an attachment matching the key [SOME_NONEXISTENT_ATTACHMENT_KEY] for record with id [RECORD_ID]

WorkflowNotFound.description: Workflow ID Not Found Request Error
WorkflowNotFound.title: Not Found Error - Workflow ID Not Found

EmailNotFound.description: Email ID Not Found Request Error
EmailNotFound.title: Bad Request Error - Email ID Not Found

AttachmentNotFound.title: Bad Request Error - attachmentId Not Found

id.description: The unique identifier of the entity

ironcladId.description: The unique Ironclad identifier of the entity

lastUpdated.description: The last updated date of the entity

namedTypeIds.description: The unique identifiers of the relationship types associated with the entity

parentId.description: The unique identifier of the parent entity

id.description: The unique identifier of the obligation

ironcladId.description: Readable Ironclad ID of the obligation

lastUpdated.description: The last updated date of the obligation

namedTypeIds.description: The unique identifiers of the types associated with the obligation. These IDs are tied with the Named Type of obligations, like 'Breach Notice', 'Compliance' and so on.

parentReadableId.description: The parent readable id of the obligation

parentRecordName.description: The parent record name of the obligation

RelationshipTypeKey.description: The key of the relationship type to be added to the entity. The key must match a valid relationship type in the company settings. The key is displayed on the relationship types page in Data Manager and is always a single word without spaces that starts with a lowercase. For now, only one relationship type can be added or updated per entity. More info on relationship types can be found in the [Entity Relationship Types](https://support.ironcladapp.com/hc/en-us/articles/28005038398231-Create-Manage-Relationship-Types-and-Entity-Properties) article.

ObligationTypeKey.description: The key of the obligation type to be added to the obligation. Only one obligation type can be added or updated per obligation, currently. More info on obligation types can be found in the [Obligation Types](https://support.ironcladapp.com/hc/en-us/articles/31128654301079-Create-and-Manage-Obligations) article.

WebhookCreateResponse400.description: Bad request error when creating webhook.

AttachmentDeleteResponse404.description: Bad request error when deleting attachment.

AsyncWorkflowLaunchResponse200.description: The response of the initiated async workflow launch.

AsyncWorkflowStatusResponse200.description: The status of the workflow launch.

schema.description: The current status of the launch workflow job.
schema.title: Workflow Launch Status

RecordAttachmentResponse400.description: Bad request error for creating record attachment.

message.example: reason why something has gone wrong

value.message: invalid authentication token
token has been revoked
token has expired

WorkflowId.name: id
WorkflowId.description: The unique identifier of a workflow

WorkflowOrIroncladId.name: id
WorkflowOrIroncladId.description: The unique identifier or Ironclad ID of a workflow.

QueryPageNumber.name: page
QueryPageNumber.description: The page number used when paginating through a list of results.

QueryPageSize.name: pageSize
QueryPageSize.description: A limit of the number of results to return.

QuerySortDirection.name: sortDirection
QuerySortDirection.description: The direction to sort by. Default is 'DESC'.

QuerySortField.name: sortField
QuerySortField.description: The field to sort by. Default is 'name'.

HydrateEntitiesFlag.name: hydrateEntities
HydrateEntitiesFlag.description: Indicates whether to fully hydrate related entities (true) or return minimal information (false).

RecordFilter.name: filter
RecordFilter.description:
Filter records using a formula. The record property ID should be enclosed in brackets `[ ]` and the value should be enclosed in single quotes `' '`. Record properties can be identified using the [Retrieve Records Schema](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-records-metadata) endpoint. Supported formula operations include:
  • Equals
  • Contains
  • And
  • Or

EntityFilter.name: filter
EntityFilter.description:
Filter entities using a formula. The entity property ID should be enclosed in brackets `[ ]` and the value should be enclosed in single quotes `' '`. Entity properties can be identified using the [Get All Entity Relationship Types](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/get-all-entity-relationship-types) endpoint. Supported formula operations include:
  • Equals
  • Contains
  • And
  • Or

ObligationFilter.name: filter
ObligationFilter.description:
Filter obligations using a formula. The obligation property ID should be enclosed in brackets `[ ]` and the value should be enclosed in single quotes `' '`. Supported formula operations include:
  • Equals
  • Contains
  • And
  • Or

RecordType.name: types
RecordType.description: Comma separated (no spaces) record types to export. Use the [Retrieve Records Schema](https://developer.ironcladapp.com/reference/list-all-records-metadata) endpoint to retrieve a list of types.

XAsUserEmail.name: x-as-user-email
XAsUserEmail.description: Denotes the actor of the request. When used, the API will take into account this user's permissions and access. This or `x-as-user-id` is required when the associated token was produced from the Client Credentials grant or with legacy bearer tokens on select endpoints. More information about [permissions](https://support.ironcladapp.com/s/article/Understanding-and-Managing-Permissions).

XAsUserId.name: x-as-user-id
XAsUserId.description: Denotes the actor of the request. When used, the API will take into account this user's permissions and access. This or `x-as-user-email` is required when the associated token was produced from the Client Credentials grant or with legacy bearer tokens on select endpoints. More information about [permissions](https://support.ironcladapp.com/s/article/Understanding-and-Managing-Permissions).

addressAsObject.name: addressAsObject
addressAsObject.description: When true, returns addresses as objects instead of strings.

UseDefaultValuesOnLaunch.name: useDefaultValues
UseDefaultValuesOnLaunch.description: If values are not specified for a given field, use the default values specified in the launch form configuration.

EmailThreadId.name: emailThreadId
EmailThreadId.description: The unique identifier of an email thread

WebhookId.name: id
WebhookId.description: The unique identifier of the webhook
